items.note: Washington's first concrete deliverable from the Lake Lucerne framework was a Treasury license permitting Iranian crude and petrochemical sales through August 21, front-loading economic relief before a public verification sequence exists; Tehran disputed the IAEA-inspection premise and rejected the US framing of how its assets would be spent, so relief moved in the one domain the US controls directly while the deal split on everything else.

Traffic is returning — CENTCOM counted 55 merchant ships carrying more than 17 million barrels in one day, stranded VLCCs are exiting and QatarEnergy-linked LNG tankers have ballasted back into the Gulf — but Iran is rebuilding passage as an administered privilege: PGSA permits filed 48 hours ahead, one designated route near Larak, mandatory insurance free during the 60-day window, and fees reserved for later.

El Obeid remains inside the prevention window — pre-entry in the wire and UN record, not pre-atrocity — after ten consecutive days of RSF drone strikes and encirclement; the Security Council has demanded the RSF halt an imminent assault on a city the Human Rights Council says puts roughly 500,000 civilians at risk, with the El Fasher playbook of reinforcement and aerial pressure running again before the ground push.

VICTUS HAZE joined fast launch to orbital rendezvous-and-proximity operations — Rocket Lab's Electron lifted off 16 hours 42 minutes after a Space Force Notice to Launch — turning responsiveness into a contest over who can get near whom first; the public record supports an autonomy-adjacent counterspace stack, not yet proof of onboard AI engagement decisions.

items.what: The post-October ceasefire toll keeps rising; a contested video disputes Israel's claim that the killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah had Hamas affiliation, which remains without confirmed public evidence — one named casualty inside a toll the lull has not stopped.

Russia is now discussing fuel imports as Ukrainian deep strikes bite into refining capacity, while Crimea restricts public life — the long-range strike campaign reaching the supply chain behind the front rather than only the front itself.

Helsing's autonomy push aside, no independent evaluation of Sakana's Fugu Ultra has appeared and Anthropic has not named the Qwable distill — the frontier claims outrunning the verification that would let anyone price them.
Brent settled below $79 as Hormuz traffic resumed, the screen pricing returning barrels rather than a verdict that the route's war-risk has cleared.
A bond sale of more than $20bn pushes the post-IPO debt story to the front of the financing question — scale funded increasingly on the balance sheet rather than on equity alone.
